recent_trades.summary: Trimming META from 6% to 4% — stock continues to drift lower near 30-day lows at $627.76 with -0.55% unrealized loss, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: META has been weak, trading near the bottom of its 30-day range ($626.62-$634.46). Already trimmed from 8% to 6% yesterday. Reducing further to free cash for stronger names. Will re-add if it reclaims $632+.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AVGO from 6.3% to 8% on strong institutional sentiment (Steve Cohen top pick) and AI infrastructure positioning, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Multiple positive headlines about AVGO as a top institutional pick and AI infrastructure play. Stock is +0.2% today with positive momentum. 30-day high is $448.58 vs current $445.58 — close to breakout. Adding ~$2,000 worth.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Broadcom weight to 18% following a wave of bullish coverage citing its pivotal role in the next generation of AI infrastructure.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom is experiencing highly positive news sentiment, being dubbed a top, safe long-term buy for AI infrastructure. Reallocating the capital freed from Oracle into AVGO maximizes our exposure to a high-momentum, high-conviction secular winner while remaining under the 20% concentration limit.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Oracle to lock in a 6.8% gain amid negative news sentiment and rising competitive pressures in the enterprise software space.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle's sentiment has turned negative, with recent headlines highlighting increasing competition from players like Palantir challenging core enterprise rules. By fully exiting this position, we secure our unrealized profits and free up capital ahead of pending macroeconomic data.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming RTX again as it continues to lag our stronger industrial and tech holdings while sitting below its recent highs, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: RTX is one of the weakest charts in the current book, showing slight negative intraday pressure and still failing to reclaim the upper end of its 30-day range near 179. We already reduced it recently, and the lack of positive news follow-through versus stronger names like GE and tech winners argues for another modest de-risking rather than a full exit.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ORCL entirely due to negative sentiment, lack of positive catalysts, and mild underperformance versus tech peers.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL headlines are unrelated or negative (Palantir competition, AI speed concerns); chart shows recent red close near lows. Position is only 3.11% so full exit is clean reallocation; no recent trades on it so this is not redundant.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AVGO to 8% target on positive AI infrastructure headlines, safe-stock status, and +0.2% intraday gain with green candle.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has the strongest sentiment and relevant news among covered names; technicals show steady uptrend within 30D range. Current 4.15% weight allows room to add without concentration risk; this rotates capital from weaker names while respecting recent trim.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating AVGO on strong AI infrastructure positioning, positive sentiment (+0.21), and multiple analyst endorsements including top Steve Cohen stock designation.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has strong positive sentiment (+0.21) with headlines highlighting its positioning for the next big shift in AI infrastructure, endorsement as a top Steve Cohen stock, and designation as a safe long-term buy. The stock is near its 30-day high. With 6.3% cash available and room for a new position, initiating at 6% target weight provides meaningful AI infrastructure diversification alongside NVDA. Markets are at record highs supporting continued tech momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Apple as shares hit new all-time intraday and closing highs amidst broad tech sector strength.
recent_trades.reasoning: With the broader market hitting record highs and news citing Apple breaking out to all-time highs, establishing a 5% baseline weight allows us to participate directly in the mega-cap tech leadership driving the major indices.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Nvidia exposure to 10% as the hot money cycle continues to heavily favor AI momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: Nvidia remains the primary beneficiary of the ongoing AI hardware investment cycle, supported by robust market breadth. Expanding our baseline position to 10% leverages the broad market strength and record Nasdaq highs.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Broadcom to a 15% weight driven by highly positive sentiment around its AI infrastructure positioning and hedge fund accumulation.
recent_trades.reasoning: Multiple recent news catalysts highlight AVGO as a top pick for the next shift in AI infrastructure and note prominent investor accumulation. We are utilizing capital freed from our defense exits to boost this position from 10.2% to 15%.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating RTX Corp to fully remove defense sector exposure following headlines of an easing Iran conflict.
recent_trades.reasoning: Similar to LMT, RTX's near-term upside is capped by the prospect of peace in the Middle East. Exiting this 7.89% position frees up significant capital to deploy into the ongoing AI and semiconductor rally.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Lockheed Martin completely as news of potential geopolitical de-escalation in the Middle East reduces the near-term premium for defense stocks.
recent_trades.reasoning: With macroeconomic headlines citing hopes for the Iran war ending, defense contractors are likely to face selling pressure as the geopolitical risk premium compresses. Reallocating this 9.89% weight entirely to stronger technology trends.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to NVDA as positive AI-related news and a tight consolidation near the top of its recent range support continuation, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is sitting just below its 30-day high with the strongest sentiment among the mega-cap tech names provided, and the recent headlines continue to reinforce AI leadership. The current position is relatively small at 4.49%, so increasing toward 7% improves exposure to a top momentum theme without becoming oversized.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing NVDA on strong positive sentiment and Jensen Huang's bold new market-cap prediction.

recent_trades.reasoning: Nvidia continues to dominate the AI infrastructure space, and CEO Jensen Huang's recent bullish market cap predictions reinforce conviction. We are deploying capital freed up from our financial exits to capture Nvidia's unparalleled pricing power and demand visibility.

recent_trades.reasoning: Goldman Sachs' COO recently called inflation the single biggest risk to the economy. We are fully exiting our 4% position to reallocate capital into secular growth themes, specifically AI hardware, which are less sensitive to macroeconomic cyclicality.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting NEE entirely after mild underperformance and lack of positive catalysts today, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEE is down 0.5% with the weakest technical picture among utilities and no supportive headlines in this run. Realizing the small 3.73% position frees capital for higher-conviction tech/healthcare ideas. Avoids repeating recent trades on other names.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMD to 5% target on its 4.5% outperformance and AI chip momentum, confidence 7/10.

Position was only 1.05% so this brings it closer to peer weights without exceeding 20% cap. No recent trade on AMD so this is not redundant.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 2% position in PepsiCo at the 30-day low on positive sentiment and defensive value, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: PEP is trading at its 30-day low of $145.77 with a sentiment score of +0.33. It offers a stable consumer staple hedge against inflation and market uncertainty. The stock typically rebounds from these levels, and the yield adds a margin of safety. We prefer this over other beaten-down names due to brand strength and consistent cash flows.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Oracle slightly after an 8.6% gain to fund a new defensive position, confidence 6/10.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.41%, Nasdaq +0.57%, Dow +0.30%. Technology led sectors (+2.76%) while Consumer Staples lagged (-1.90%). GPT-5.4 led the day at +1.14%. 6 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek V4 Pro at +22.79%. Key headline: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due - Investor's Business Daily — broadly positive for equities.
